[M] In cross country, men's swimming and diving, indoor and outdoor track and field, and men's wrestling, an institution shall schedule and play at least 50 percent of its contests against Division I opponents to satisfy the minimum number of contests specified in Bylaw 20.9.6.3. (Adopted: 4/29/04 effective 8/1/04, Revised: 1/15/11 effective 8/1/11, 6/3/13, 4/25/18 effective 8/1/18)

title: Application of Scheduling Requirements -- Sports Other Than Football and Basketball
Reclassifying Opponents
verbatimText: [M] A reclassifying institution shall be counted as a Division I opponent in the year the reclassifying institution must comply with Division I scheduling requirements (year two of reclassifying). A reclassifying institution may be counted as a Division I opponent in year one of the reclassification process if the reclassifying institution meets Division I scheduling requirements. (Adopted: 1/10/92 effective 9/1/04, Revised: 1/11/94, 6/3/13, 1/23/19 effective 8/1/19) 20.9.7.3.2 Multiteam Events -- Individual Sports Other Than Cross Country and Indoor and Outdoor Track and Field. [M] In individual sports other than cross country and indoor and outdoor track and field, if a multiteam event is not scored by division, an institution may use the event to satisfy the scheduling requirements, provided at least two-thirds of the institutions competing in the event are Division I members. If a multiteam event is 6/22/26 369 scored by division, there is no minimum required percentage of participating Division I institutions. Regardless of whether a multiteam event is scored by division, an institution that is using a multiteam event to satisfy the scheduling requirements must satisfy the minimum required number of participants, per Bylaw 20.9.6.3. (Adopted: 1/10/92 effective 9/1/94, Revised: 1/11/94, 6/3/13, 4/25/18 effective 8/1/18)

title: Multiteam Events -- Cross Country and Indoor and Outdoor Track and Field
verbatimText: [M] In cross country and indoor and outdoor track and field, if a multiteam event is not scored by division, an institution may use the event to satisfy the scheduling requirements, provided at least one other institution competing in the event is a Division I member. If a multiteam event is scored by division, there is no minimum required percentage of participating Division I institutions. Regardless of whether a multiteam event is scored by division, an institution that is using a multiteam event to satisfy the scheduling requirements must satisfy the minimum required number of participants, per Bylaw 20.9.6.3. (Adopted: 4/25/18 effective 8/1/18)

title: Exception
verbatimText: [M] The scheduling criteria in Bylaws 20.9.7.1 and 20.9.7.2 shall not apply in sports in which the only championship opportunity is the National Collegiate Championship per Bylaw 18.02.1.1 or sports in which there is no NCAA-sponsored postseason championship. (Adopted: 1/10/91 effective 9/1/94, Revised: 1/9/96 effective 8/1/96, 6/3/13)

title: Waiver -- Situations Beyond Institutional Control
verbatimText: [M] The Membership Committee, by a twothirds majority of its members present and voting, may approve waivers of the scheduling requirement in situations beyond the control of the institution (e.g., weather conditions or natural disasters) that prevent the completion of scheduled competition. (Adopted: 1/9/96, Revised: 11/1/07 effective 8/1/08, 6/3/13, 8/7/14, 10/4/17, 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Four-Game Limit
verbatimText: [M] An institution may schedule and play not more than four basketball games, including any contest (e.g., scrimmage, exhibition), in an academic year against institutions that are not members of Division I. (Revised: 3/1/12)

title: One-Third of Contests in Home Arena
verbatimText: [M] An active or reclassifying member must play at least one-third of its regular-season basketball contests in the arena regularly used for the institution's home games. (Revised: 1/11/94 effective 9/2/94, 3/10/04, 1/15/11 effective 8/1/11)

title: Multiple Home Arenas
verbatimText: [M] In meeting the home-arena requirement, an institution may use more than one arena, provided each arena is located within a 30-mile radius of the institution's main campus and each arena is used annually by the institution for at least two home basketball contests.

title: One-Third of Women's Contests Away From Home or at a Neutral Site
verbatimText: [M] An active member or a reclassifying member must play at least one-third of its regular-season women's basketball contests away from home or at a neutral site. A reclassifying member is required to apply scheduling criteria beginning with year two of the reclassifying process. (Adopted: 1/15/11 effective 8/1/11)

title: Membership Classification of Opponents
verbatimText: [M] In determining whether an institution meets the scheduling criteria, each opponent shall be counted as it was classified on August 1 of the academic year involved.

title: Reclassifying Opponents
verbatimText: [M] A reclassifying institution shall be counted as a Division I opponent in the year the reclassifying institution must comply with Division I scheduling requirements (year two of the reclassifying process). A reclassifying institution may be counted as a Division I opponent in year one of the reclassification process if the reclassifying institution meets Division I scheduling requirements. (Adopted: 4/15/97, Revised: 4/24/03 effective 8/1/03, 1/23/19 effective 8/1/19)

title: Waiver
verbatimText: [M] The Men's Basketball Oversight Committee or Women's Basketball Oversight Committee, by a two-thirds majority of its members present and voting, may grant a waiver of the provisions of Bylaw 20.9.8.4.1 in cases of reclassification of an opponent when there is an enforceable game contract, executed in writing, or in the case of similar contractual problems. (Revised: 11/1/07 effective 8/1/08, 8/7/14, 10/4/17, 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Football Bowl Subdivision Requirements
verbatimText: [FBS] An institution classified in the Football Bowl Subdivision shall meet the additional requirements listed below. (Revised: 12/15/06)

title: Sports Sponsorship
verbatimText: [FBS] The institution shall sponsor a minimum of 16 varsity intercollegiate sports, including football, based on the minimum sports sponsorship and scheduling requirements set forth in Bylaws 20.9.6 and 20.9.6.3, including a minimum of six sports involving all-male teams or mixed teams of males and females, and a minimum 6/22/26 370 of eight varsity intercollegiate sports (of which a maximum of two emerging sports per Bylaw 20.02.7 may be used) based on the minimum sports sponsorship and scheduling requirements set forth in Bylaws 20.9.6 and 20.9.6.3 and involving allfemale teams, subject to the waiver provision in Bylaw 20.9.6.2. (Revised: 1/11/94 effective 9/1/94, 4/25/02 effective 8/1/04)

title: Football Scheduling Requirements
verbatimText: [FBS] The institution shall schedule and play at least 60 percent of its football games against members of the Football Bowl Subdivision. The institution shall schedule and play at least five regular-season home games against Football Bowl Subdivision opponents. For purposes of satisfying the home-games requirement, a contest shall be considered a home contest if it is played in the stadium in which an institution conducts at least 50 percent of its home contests. In addition, an institution may use one home contest against a Football Bowl Subdivision member conducted at a neutral site to satisfy the home-games requirement. (Revised: 4/25/02 effective 8/1/04, 1/12/04, 12/15/06, 10/6/17)

title: Exception -- Football Championship Subdivision Opponent
verbatimText: [FBS] Each year, a Football Bowl Subdivision institution may count one contest against a Football Championship Subdivision opponent to satisfy the football-scheduling requirement specified in Bylaw 20.9.9.2, provided the Football Championship Subdivision opponent has averaged at least 56.7 grants-in-aid per year in football over a rolling two-year period. (Adopted: 4/28/05, Revised: 12/15/06, 5/18/22, 9/21/22 effective 8/1/23, 6/6/25 effective 7/1/25)

title: Membership Classification of Opponents
verbatimText: [FBS] In determining whether an institution meets the scheduling requirements of the Football Bowl Subdivision, each opponent shall be counted as it was classified on September 1 of the academic year involved. (Revised: 12/15/06, 10/6/17)

title: Reclassifying Opponents
verbatimText: [FBS] A reclassifying institution shall be counted as a Football Bowl Subdivision opponent in the year the reclassifying institution must meet the Football Bowl Subdivision scheduling requirements (year two of the reclassifying process). A reclassifying institution may be counted as a Football Bowl Subdivision opponent in year one of the reclassification process if the reclassifying institution meets Football Bowl Subdivision scheduling requirements. (Adopted: 4/15/97, Revised: 3/10/04, 12/15/06, 1/23/19 effective 8/1/19)

title: Waiver
verbatimText: [FBS] The Football Bowl Subdivision Oversight Committee, by a two-thirds majority of its members present and voting, may grant a waiver of the provisions of Bylaw 20.9.9.2.2 in cases of reclassification of an opponent when there is an enforceable game contract, executed in writing, or in the case of similar contractual problems. (Revised: 12/15/06, 11/1/07 effective 8/1/08, 8/7/14, 10/4/17, 11/7/23)

title: Canceled Games
verbatimText: [FBS] A canceled game shall not be counted toward meeting the Football Bowl Subdivision scheduling requirements unless the Football Bowl Subdivision Oversight Committee, by a two-thirds majority of its members present and voting, approve a waiver of the criterion in a situation in which: (Revised: 12/15/06, 11/1/07 effective 8/1/08, 8/7/14, 10/4/17, 11/7/23) (a) The member institution can document that it had scheduled (for that academic year) the appropriate minimum percentage of contests with members of the Football Bowl Subdivision, under enforceable game contracts executed in writing; (b) An opponent canceled a game that it had contracted to play that academic year; and (c) Despite a good-faith effort, the institution was unable to re-arrange its schedule to play the appropriate percentage of contests with members of the Football Bowl Subdivision.

title: Alaska, Hawaii or Puerto Rico
verbatimText: [FBS] Games played in Alaska, Hawaii or Puerto Rico shall not be counted for purposes of meeting the subdivision's scheduling requirement if such games are exempt from counting toward the playing-season limitations. (Adopted: 1/10/92)

title: Financial Aid Requirements
verbatimText: [FBS] The institution shall satisfy the following financial aid requirements: (Adopted: 4/25/02 effective 8/1/04, Revised: 10/4/23 effective 8/1/27 for current members of FBS or institutions that initiated the reclassification process by 6/1/23; effective upon conclusion of the two-year reclassification period for institutions applying to reclassify to FBS after 6/1/23, 6/6/25 effective 7/1/25) (a) Award an average of at least 90 percent of the permissible maximum number of overall grants-in-aid ( based on the applicable limits in 2024-25) per year during a rolling two-year period in all sports used to meet the sports sponsorship requirement (Bylaw 20.9.9.1); and (b) Annually award a minimum of 210 athletics grants-in-aids and expend at least $6 million on grants-in-aid to studentathletes.

title: Type of Financial Aid Counted
verbatimText: [FBS] The institution shall count only athletically related financial aid (as defined in Bylaw 15.02.4.1) awarded to student-athletes. (Adopted: 3/10/04 effective 8/1/04, Revised: 6/6/25 effective 7/1/25) 6/22/26 371

title: Exception -- National Service Academies
verbatimText: [FBS] The national service academies are exempt from all financial aid requirements set forth in Bylaw 20.9.9. (Adopted: 4/25/02 effective 8/1/04)

title: Noncompliance with Football Bowl Subdivision Criteria
verbatimText: [FBS]

title: Notice of Noncompliance
verbatimText: [FBS] An institution that fails to satisfy any of the Football Bowl Subdivision membership requirements shall receive notice of such noncompliance. After receiving notice, any further noncompliance with the Football Bowl Subdivision requirements within a 10-year period shall cause the institution to be placed in restricted membership. (Adopted: 8/5/04, Revised: 12/15/06)

title: Restricted Membership
verbatimText: [FBS] While in restricted membership, the institution shall not be eligible for postseason football competition. At the conclusion of the one-year period, the institution shall be granted membership in its preferred subdivision, provided the institution complies with the subdivision's criteria. If the member does not meet the criteria of any subdivision at the end of the restricted membership period, the institution may continue to be classified as a Division I member in sports other than football, provided the institution satisfies the Division I membership requirements. A Division I member that loses Football Bowl Subdivision status must comply with the multidivision classification requirements set forth in Bylaw 20.5 to regain such status. (Adopted: 8/5/04, Revised: 12/15/06)

title: Waivers
verbatimText: [FBS] There shall be no waivers of the Football Bowl Subdivision membership requirements. (Adopted: 4/24/03 effective 8/1/03, Revised: 12/15/06)

title: Football Championship Subdivision Requirements
verbatimText: [FCSD] An institution classified as a Football Championship Subdivision member shall meet the additional requirements listed below. (Revised: 12/15/06)

title: Sports Sponsorship
verbatimText: [FCSD] The institution shall sponsor in Division I a minimum of: (Revised: 1/10/91 effective 9/1/94, 1/11/94 effective 9/1/94, 1/15/11) (a) Seven varsity intercollegiate sports, including football, based on the minimum requirements of Bylaws 20.9.6 and 20.9.6.3 and involving all-male teams or mixed teams of males and females, and seven varsity intercollegiate sports (of which a maximum of two emerging sports per Bylaw 20.02.7 may be utilized) based on the minimum requirements of Bylaws 20.9.6 and 20.9.6.3 and involving all-female teams, subject to the waiver provision in Bylaw 20.9.6.2; or (b) Six varsity intercollegiate sports, including football, based on the minimum requirements of Bylaws 20.9.6 and 20.9.6.3 and involving all-male teams or mixed teams of males and females, and eight varsity intercollegiate sports (of which a maximum of two emerging sports per Bylaw 20.02.7 may be utilized) based on the minimum requirements of Bylaws 20.9.6 and 20.9.6.3 and involving all-female teams, subject to the waiver provision in Bylaw 20.9.6.2.

title: Football Scheduling Requirement
verbatimText: [FCSD] The institution shall schedule and play more than 50 percent of its football games against Football Bowl Subdivision or Football Championship Subdivision members. (Revised: 12/15/06)

title: Membership Classification of Opponents
verbatimText: [FCSD] In determining whether an institution meets the scheduling criteria of the Football Championship Subdivision, each opponent shall be counted as it was classified on September 1 of the academic year involved. (Revised: 12/15/06, 1/15/11)

title: Reclassifying Opponents
verbatimText: [FCSD] A reclassifying institution shall be counted as a Football Championship Subdivision opponent in the year the reclassifying institution must comply with Football Championship Subdivision scheduling requirements (year two of the reclassifying process). A reclassifying institution may be counted as a Football Championship Subdivision opponent in year one of the reclassification process if the reclassifying institution meets Football Championship Subdivision scheduling requirements. (Adopted: 4/15/97, Revised: 3/10/04, 12/15/06, 1/15/11, 1/23/19 effective 8/1/19)

title: Waiver
verbatimText: [FCSD] The Football Championship Subdivision Oversight Committee, by a two-thirds majority of those present and voting, may grant a waiver of the provisions of Bylaw 20.9.10.2.1 in cases of reclassification of an opponent when there is an enforceable game contract, executed in writing, or in the case of similar contractual problems. (Revised: 12/15/06, 1/15/11, 8/7/14, 10/4/17, 11/7/23)

title: Canceled Games
verbatimText: [FCSD] A canceled game shall not be counted toward meeting the Football Championship Subdivision scheduling criterion unless the Football Championship Subdivision Oversight Committee, by a two-thirds majority of those present and voting, approves a waiver of the criterion in a situation in which: (Revised: 12/15/06, 1/15/11, 8/7/14, 10/4/17, 11/7/23) (a) The member institution can document that it had scheduled (for that academic year) the appropriate minimum percentage of contests with members of the Football Championship Subdivision, under enforceable game contracts executed in writing; 6/22/26 372 (b) An opponent canceled a game that it had contracted to play that academic year; and (c) Despite a good-faith effort, the institution was unable to rearrange its schedule to play the appropriate percentage of contests with Football Championship Subdivision members.

title: Alaska, Hawaii or Puerto Rico
verbatimText: [FCSD] Games played in Alaska, Hawaii or Puerto Rico shall not be counted for purposes of meeting the subdivision's scheduling requirement if such games are exempt from counting toward the playing-season limitations. (Adopted: 1/10/92, Revised: 1/15/11)

title: Geographical   Waiver   --   Football   Championship   Subdivision
verbatimText: [FCSD] The Football Championship Subdivision Oversight Committee, by a two-thirds majority of those present and voting, may waive the provisions of Bylaw 20.9.10.2 for a Football Championship Subdivision institution that does not offer athletically related financial aid in football, or which offers fewer than 20 percent of the maximum allowable number of scholarships in the Football Championship Subdivision and was classified in Division II prior to September 1993, if it is determined that fewer than six other championship subdivision football programs exist within a 500-mile radius of the institution's campus that do not offer athletically related financial aid in football or which offer fewer than 20 percent of the maximum allowable number of scholarships in the Football Championship Subdivision and were classified in Division II prior to September 1993. An institution must calculate the percentage based upon the amount of athletically related financial aid received by its student-athletes, as opposed to including all institutional aid. (Adopted: 1/16/93 effective 8/1/93, Revised: 1/10/95, 12/15/06, 1/15/11, 8/7/14, 10/4/17, 11/7/23) 6/22/26 373 BYLAW, ARTICLE 21 Governance Structure and Committees

title: Faculty Athletics Representative
verbatimText: A faculty athletics representative is a member of an institution's faculty or administrative staff who is designated by the institution's president or chancellor or other appropriate entity to represent the institution and its faculty in the institution's relationships with the NCAA and its conference, if any (see also Bylaw 8.1.3). (Adopted: 1/9/96 effective 8/1/97, Revised: 6/26/19 effective 8/1/19, 1/20/22)

title: Institutional Senior Woman Administrator
verbatimText: [#] An institutional senior woman administrator is the highest-ranking female involved in the management of an institution's intercollegiate athletics program. An institution with a female director of athletics may designate a different woman involved with the management of the member's program as a fifth representative to the NCAA governance structure. (Revised: 11/1/01 effective 8/1/02, 10/27/05, 1/20/22, 2/17/23, 1/28/26)

title: Conference Senior Woman Administrator
verbatimText: A conference senior woman administrator is the highestranking female involved with the conduct and policy processes of a conference's office. A conference with a female commissioner may designate a different woman involved with the management of the conference as a representative to the NCAA governance structure. (Adopted: 11/1/01 effective 8/1/02, Revised: 10/27/05, 1/20/22, 2/17/23, 1/28/26)

title: Eligibility for Service
verbatimText: Individuals serving on Division I committees, or as Division I representatives on Association-wide or common committees shall be salaried on a regular basis by a Division I institution or conference and perform a regular staff function representing at least 50 percent of the normal workload for a staff member at that institution or conference, unless otherwise specified. In addition, a conference office staff member must be employed at a single or multisport conference that meets the requirements for automatic qualification. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Modification in Employment Status
verbatimText: If a committee member's employment status is altered to the extent that the individual is not eligible for service, the individual shall be replaced. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Waiver of Replacement Requirement
verbatimText: The Cabinet Subcommittee may waive the replacement requirement or approve a delayed replacement if it deems that an immediate replacement would be detrimental to the work of the involved committee. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, Revised: 10/22/25)

title: Individuals on Sabbatical or Temporary Leave
verbatimText: An individual on sabbatical or other temporary leave for a period not exceeding 12 consecutive months may be considered to be eligible for committee service. An individual on terminal leave or on leave in excess of 12 consecutive months shall not be eligible to serve on a committee. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Exception -- Secretary-Rules Editor
verbatimText: An individual serving as a secretary-rules editor shall not be subject to the eligibility for service requirement. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Exception -- Sport Oversight Committees and Playing Rules Subcommittees
verbatimText: Institutional staff members from Division II or Division III institutions that sponsor a Division I sport may serve on that sport's oversight committee or playing rules subcommittee as a Division I representative. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Selection to Committees
verbatimText: Unless otherwise specified, committee members shall be selected by the entities they represent. At-large members shall be selected from eligible nominees by the committee with the vacancy. Committee policies and procedures may provide additional specificity. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Term of Office of Committees
verbatimText: The term of office for members of committees shall be as follows: (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25) (a) Terms of office for guaranteed conference or subdivisional positions shall be determined by the conference or subdivision; (b) Unless otherwise specified, an at-large member or a member appointed based on basketball success shall be appointed for one four-year term. A former at-large committee member or a member appointed based on basketball success may be appointed to an additional term on the same committee after three years have elapsed. A member appointed based on sport success other than basketball success is appointed to a four-year term with the option of being immediately appointed to an additional four-year term at the end of each term if the member’s conference continues to qualify for appointment based on sport success. A member of a rules subcommittee shall be appointed to one four-year term with the option to be 6/22/26 374 immediately appointed to an additional four-year term. The applicable appointing entity may approve extensions of terms for extenuating circumstances (e.g., staggering of terms, etc.); (c) Unless otherwise specified, a term of office shall commence on the first day of September following the member's appointment; and (d) An at-large member who serves more than one-half of a term shall be considered to have served a full term. An at-large member who serves one-half of a term or less shall be eligible for reappointment for up to a full term (in addition to time served before reappointment) at the discretion of the appointing entity.

title: Association-Wide Committees
verbatimText: [#] Association-wide committees deal with issues that affect all members of the Association and perform duties necessary to the on-going operation of the Association. Association-wide committees are comprised of members from each of the Association’s divisions. (Adopted: 1/14/97 effective 8/1/97, Revised: 1/20/22 effective 8/1/22)

title: Common Committees
verbatimText: [#] Common committees deal with issues that apply to more than one division of the Association. Common committees perform duties necessary to the on-going operation of the applicable divisions and are comprised of members from the applicable divisions. (Adopted: 1/14/97 effective 8/1/97, Revised: 1/20/22 effective 8/1/22)

title: Districts
verbatimText: [#] For purposes of committee composition, the geographical districts are as follows: (Adopted: 10/30/03) (a) District 1 -- Connecticut, Maine, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, Rhode Island, Vermont; (b) District 2 -- Delaware, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Puerto Rico, West Virginia; (c) District 3 -- Alabama, District of Columbia, Florida, Georgia, Kentucky, Louisiana, Maryland, Mississippi, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia; (d) District 4 -- Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Minnesota, Ohio, Wisconsin; (e) District 5 -- Iowa, Kansas, Missouri, Nebraska, North Dakota, Oklahoma, South Dakota; (f) District 6 -- Arkansas, New Mexico, Texas; (g) District 7 -- Arizona, Colorado, Idaho, Montana, Utah, Wyoming; and (h) District 8 -- Alaska, British Columbia, California, Hawaii, Nevada, Oregon, Washington.

title: Conflict of Interest
verbatimText: A committee member shall not participate in the committee's discussion or vote on any action that might bring direct or indirect financial benefit to the member of any organization in which the member is financially interested (other than the member's institution or the conference of which it is a member). A violation of this rule by a member of a committee shall not invalidate the action taken by the committee if, following disclosure of the conflict of interest, the committee authorizes, ratifies or approves the action by a vote sufficient for the purpose, without counting the vote of the committee member with the conflict of interest, and the Council approves such action. All committee members shall agree to this policy prior to committee service and shall abide by the policy at all times. The current conflict of interest policy is located on the NCAA website (NCAA.org) or may be obtained from the NCAA national office. (Adopted: 1/13/09, Revised: 8/7/14)

title: Composition
verbatimText: The Board of Directors shall include 14 voting members and three nonvoting members. The majority of the voting members shall be presidents or chancellors. The nonvoting members shall include the NCAA president, a Division I member of the Executive Committee of the Faculty Athletics Representatives Association, selected by the Executive Committee of the Faculty Athletics Representatives Association, and the chair of the Cabinet. The voting members of the Board shall include: (Adopted: 1/9/96 effective 8/1/97, Revised: 1/14/97 effective 8/1/97, 8/5/99, 4/24/03, 11/1/07 effective 8/1/05, 5/2/13 effective 8/1/13, 8/7/14, 1/20/22, 4/26/23 effective 8/1/23, 4/22/24 effective 8/2/24, 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, 10/22/25, 3/12/26 effective 2/25/26) (a) One representative from each of the following conferences: (1) Atlantic Coast Conference; (3) Big Ten Conference; and (2) Big 12 Conference; (4) Southeastern Conference. (b) Two representatives from among the other Football Bowl Subdivision conferences (limited to one individual from any one conference). (c) Two representatives from among the Football Championship Subdivision conferences (limited to one individual from any one conference). (d) Two representatives from among the Basketball Subdivision conferences (limited to one individual from any one conference). 6/22/26 375 (e) One representative from either the Football Championship Subdivision conferences or the Basketball Subdivision conferences. (f) The chair of the Student-Athlete Advocacy Committee, a current or former football student-athlete and a current or former student-athlete in men’s or women’s basketball.

title: Selection
verbatimText: The voting members of the Board of Directors other than the basketball and football student-athletes shall be selected by the entities they represent. The process for selection of the football and basketball student-athletes shall be set forth in the Board of Directors’ policies and procedures. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Duties and Responsibilities
verbatimText: The Board of Directors shall serve as the overall governing body for Division I, with the authority to ratify, amend or defeat any legislative or policy decision made by the Cabinet. The board shall have responsibility for financial oversight of the Division I budget and Division I litigation, and shall also serve as the final authority on academic policies and non-case specific infractions items. Specific duties and responsibilities are set forth in Board of Directors’ policies and procedures. (Adopted: 1/9/96 effective 8/1/97, Revised: 8/7/03 effective 8/1/04, 3/8/06, 11/1/07 effective 8/1/08, 10/28/10, 7/31/14, 8/7/14, 10/30/14, 10/29/15, 8/8/18 effective 8/1/19, 12/20/18, 1/23/19 effective 8/1/19, 1/20/22, 8/31/22 effective 1/1/23, 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, 10/22/25)

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) shall have 4.5 votes each. All other voting representatives shall have one vote each. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Voting Method
verbatimText: The method of voting on issues considered by the Board of Directors shall be by roll call, except for those actions taken by the unanimous consent of the Board members present and voting. Roll-call vote results shall be reported to the membership. (Adopted: 1/9/96 effective 8/1/97, Revised: 1/20/22)

title: Executive Committee
verbatimText: The Board of Directors Executive Committee is empowered to act on behalf of the Council to transact necessary and routine items of business as set forth in the Board of Directors' policies and procedures. (Adopted: 10/4/17, Revised: 1/20/22, 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Standing Committees
verbatimText: Standing committees may be formed by the Board of Directors as it deems necessary. Details related to such committees (e.g., composition, selection, duties) shall be set forth in the Board of Directors' policies and procedures. (Adopted: 8/7/14, Revised: 10/14/17, 1/20/22)

title: Division I Cabinet
verbatimText: (Revised: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, 10/22/25)

title: Composition
verbatimText: The Cabinet shall include 20 members and shall be comprised of athletics administrators (e.g., athletics directors, senior woman administrators, conference administrators, compliance administrators and other senior level administrators), faculty athletics representatives and student-athletes. The members of the Cabinet shall include: (Adopted: 1/9/96 effective 8/1/97, Revised: 1/14/97 effective 8/1/97, 8/5/99, 4/24/03, 12/15/06, 11/1/07 effective 8/1/08, 5/2/13 effective 8/1/13, 8/7/14, 1/20/22, 4/22/24 effective 8/2/24, 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, 10/22/25, 3/12/26 effective 2/25/26) (a) Two representatives from each of the following conferences: (1) Atlantic Coast Conference; (3) Big Ten Conference; and (2) Big 12 Conference; (4) Southeastern Conference. (b) Two representatives from among the following conferences: (1) American Conference; (4) Mountain West Conference; (2) Conference USA; (5) Pac-12 Conference; or (3) Mid-American Conference; (6) Sun Belt Conference. (c) Three representatives from among the following conferences: (1) Atlantic Sun (ASUN) Conference; (8) Ohio Valley Conference; (2) Big Sky Conference; (9) Patriot League; (3) Coastal Athletic Association; (10) Pioneer Football League; (4) The Ivy League; (11) Southern Conference; (5) Mid-Eastern Athletic Conference; (12) Southland Conference; or (6) Missouri Valley Football Conference; (13) Southwestern Athletic Conference. (7) Northeast Conference; 6/22/26 376 (d) Three representatives from among the following conferences: (1) America East Conference; (7) Metro Atlantic Athletic Conference; (2) Atlantic 10 Conference; (8) Missouri Valley Conference; (3) Big East Conference; (9) The Summit League; (4) Big South Conference; (10) West Coast Conference; or (5) Big West Conference; (11) Western Athletic Conference. (6) Horizon League; (e) One faculty athletics representative appointed by the Faculty Athletics Representatives Association. (f) One member of the Student-Athlete Advocacy Committee, a current or former football student-athlete and a current or former student-athlete in men’s or women’s basketball.

title: Selection
verbatimText: Members of the Cabinet other than the basketball and football student-athletes shall be selected by the entities they represent. The process for selection of the football and basketball student-athletes shall be set forth in the Cabinet's policies and procedures. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, Revised: 10/22/25)

title: Duties and Responsibilities
verbatimText: The Cabinet shall report to the Board of Directors and provide general oversight of legislation and policies and procedures that impact the administration of Division I athletics programs. Specific duties and responsibilities are set forth below and in the Cabinet's policies and procedures. (Revised: 1/9/96 effective 8/1/97, 11/1/07 effective 8/1/08, 8/7/14, 1/20/22, 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, 10/22/25) (a) Serve as the division's primary legislative authority, subject to review by the Board of Directors (see Bylaw 9.2.2); (b) Provide strategic direction on key issues impacting Division I; (c) Take final action on matters delegated to it by the Board of Directors; (d) Oversee interpretations of the bylaws generally and consider appeals of NCAA staff interpretations in areas that are not subject to the authority of other committees; (e) Oversee the processes for legislative relief waivers generally and consider appeals of NCAA staff decisions in areas that are not subject to the authority of other committees; (f) Monitor the effectiveness, operations and policies and procedures of Division I committees; (g) Oversee Division I membership requirements and processes; and (h) Elect institutions to active Division I membership and approve reclassifying institutions within Division I.

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: Voting shall be weighted as follows: (Adopted: 8/7/14, Revised: 1/20/22, 4/22/24 effective 8/2/24, 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25) (a) Representatives from the following conferences and the conference commissioner representative of these conferences shall have 2.75 votes each: (1) Atlantic Coast Conference; (3) Big Ten Conference; and (2) Big 12 Conference; (4) Southeastern Conference. (b) All other representatives shall have one vote each.

title: Voting Method
verbatimText: The method of voting on issues considered by the Cabinet shall be by roll call, except for actions taken by the unanimous consent of the members present and voting. Roll-call vote results of legislative actions shall be reported to the membership and shall include a record of each member's vote. The overall outcomes of votes on other matters shall be reported to the membership, but neither the vote count nor individual votes shall be reported. (Adopted: 1/9/96 effective 8/1/97, Revised: 11/1/07 effective 8/1/08, 10/28/10, 8/7/14, 1/20/22, 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, 10/22/25)

title: Cabinet Subcommittee
verbatimText: (Revised: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, 10/22/25)

title: Composition and Selection
verbatimText: The Cabinet Subcommittee shall consist of members of the Cabinet. The process for selection and the term of office of members shall be set forth in the policies and procedures of the Cabinet. (Adopted: 1/14/97 effective 8/1/97, Revised: 10/10/00, 12/15/06, 11/1/07 effective 8/1/08, 8/7/14, 10/14/17, 1/20/22, 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, 10/22/25)

title: Duties
verbatimText: The Cabinet Subcommittee is empowered to act on behalf of the Cabinet to transact necessary and routine items of business as set forth in the Cabinet policies and procedures. (Adopted: 1/14/97 effective 8/1/97, Revised: 11/1/07 effective 8/1/08, 8/7/14, 10/4/17, 1/20/22, 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, 10/22/25) 6/22/26 377

title: Review of Actions
verbatimText: All actions of the Cabinet Subcommittee shall be reported to and subject to review by the Cabinet. (Adopted: 1/14/97 effective 8/1/97, Revised: 11/1/07 effective 8/1/08, 8/7/14, 10/4/17, 1/20/22, 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, 10/22/25)

title: Association-Wide Committees
verbatimText: The following committees shall report to the Cabinet regarding issues related to Division I: (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, Revised: 10/22/25) (a) Committee on Competitive Safeguards and Medical Aspects of Sports; (b) Awards Committee; (c) Committee on Access, Opportunity and Impact; (d) Postgraduate Scholarship Committee; and (e) Walter Byers Scholarship Committee.

title: Academics and Eligibility Committee
verbatimText: (Revised: 8/5/25 effective 11/1/25)

title: Composition
verbatimText: The Academics and Eligibility Committee shall be composed of 21 members, including two from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a); three from among the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(b); three from among the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(c); three from among the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(d); and four studentathletes. Two representatives of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) and two representatives from among other conferences must be faculty athletics representatives. Two student-athletes shall be members of the Student-Athlete Advocacy Committee, one member shall be a current or former football student-athlete and one member shall be a current or former student-athlete in men’s or women’s basketball. (Adopted: 8/7/14, Revised: 1/20/22, 8/5/25 effective 11/1/25, 3/12/26 effective 2/25/26)

title: Selection
verbatimText: Members of the Academics and Eligibility Committee other than the basketball and football studentathletes shall be selected by the entities they represent. The process for selection of the football and basketball studentathletes shall be set forth in the Academics and Eligibility Committee’s policies and procedures. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 11/1/25)

title: Duties and Responsibilities
verbatimText: The Academics and Eligibility Committee shall report to the Cabinet and shall oversee all aspects of academics and athletics and academic eligibility. The committee has authority to take final action on routine and noncontroversial matters of general academic and athletics eligibility policy, subject to oversight by the Cabinet. Specific duties and responsibilities are set forth in the Cabinet policies and procedures. (Adopted: 8/7/14, Revised: 1/20/22, 8/5/25 effective 11/1/25, 10/22/25)

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) shall have three votes each. All other representatives shall have one vote each. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 11/1/25)

title: Subcommittees of the Academics and Eligibility Committee
verbatimText: (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 11/1/25)

title: Academic Eligibility Subcommittee
verbatimText: (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 11/1/25)

title: Composition
verbatimText: The Academic Eligibility Subcommittee shall be composed of 11 members of the Academics and Eligibility Committee: one from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a); two from among the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(b); two from among the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(c); one from among the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(d); and two student-athletes. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 11/1/25)

title: Duties and Responsibilities
verbatimText: The Academic Eligibility Subcommittee shall: (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 11/1/25, Revised: 10/22/25) (a) Study issues and make policy or legislative recommendations concerning academic matters that impact studentathletes; (b) Be responsible for review and consideration of the portions of the legislation that relate to principles of sound academic requirements; (c) With oversight from the Cabinet, make legislative and policy recommendations related to initial eligibility, twoyear college transfers, four-year college transfers, progress toward degree requirements and academic integrity; (d) Perform all duties directly related to interpretations, including determining interpretations, identifying interpretations to be incorporated into the Division I Manual, modifying interpretations, reviewing interpretations issued by the NCAA staff and responding to requests from member institutions to interpret bylaws in the areas listed in (c) above. The Cabinet shall oversee the policies and procedures for interpretations; 6/22/26 378 (e) Review and act upon appeals of NCAA staff decisions on initial eligibility, two-year college transfer and progresstoward-degree waivers, maintain associated waiver guidelines and provide an annual report on waiver decisions to the Cabinet. The subcommittee shall oversee the policies and procedures for such waivers; (f) Receive and act upon reports from the High School Review and International Student Records Committees; (g) Manage the Academic Performance Program and Graduation Passport Program; (h) Perform all duties directly related to the administration of the APP, including interpretations of APP legislation and policies, hearing appeals (or waiver requests) of institutions or teams subject to penalties or loss of access to postseason and any other matters of appeal pursuant to the legislation and policies and procedures of the APP; (i) Oversee the Academic Values-Based Revenue Distribution and monitor the academic metrics upon which distribution is based; (j) Oversee the Degree Completion Program; (k) Oversee the Accelerating Academic Success Program; (l) Maintain relationships between the Association and the nation's two-year colleges as represented by established regional and national organizations; and (m) Administer and oversee any other academic matters not otherwise specified, as directed by the Cabinet.

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) shall have 3.25 votes each. All other representatives shall have one vote each. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 11/1/25)

title: Authority
verbatimText: An interpretive or waiver decision made by the Academic Eligibility Subcommittee shall be final, binding and conclusive and shall not be subject to further review by any other authority. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 11/1/25)

title: Athletics Eligibility Subcommittee
verbatimText: (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 11/1/25)

title: Composition
verbatimText: The Athletics Eligibility Subcommittee shall be composed of 10 members of the Academics and Eligibility Committee: one from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a); one from among the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(b); one from among the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(c); two from among the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(d); and two student-athletes. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 11/1/25)

title: Duties and Responsibilities
verbatimText: The Athletics Eligibility Subcommittee shall: (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 11/1/25, Revised: 10/22/25) (a) With oversight from the Cabinet, make legislative and policy recommendations related to the period of eligibility and seasons of competition; awards, benefits and expenses unrelated to the House settlement; involvement with professional teams; preenrollment athletics eligibility; and professional sports agents; (b) Perform all duties directly related to interpretations, including determining interpretations, identifying interpretations to be incorporated into the Division I Manual, modifying interpretations, reviewing interpretations issued by the governance and member services staff and acting on appeals of staff interpretations of the bylaws in the areas listed in (a) above. The Cabinet shall oversee the policies and procedures for interpretations; (c) Maintain the previously approved request list for student-athlete reinstatement; (d) Review and act upon appeals of staff decisions on legislative relief wavier requests related to the areas listed in (a) above and provide annual reports of legislative relief waiver decisions to the Cabinet; and (e) Review and act upon appeals of staff decisions on student-athlete reinstatement requests and provide annual reports of reinstatement decisions to the Cabinet. The subcommittee shall oversee the policies and procedures for the restoration of eligibility of a student-athlete who is ineligible for intercollegiate competition.

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) shall have 2.75 votes each. All other representatives shall have one vote each. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 11/1/25)

title: Authority
verbatimText: An interpretive, waiver or reinstatement decision made by the Athletics Eligibility Subcommittee shall be final, binding and conclusive and shall not be subject to further review by any other authority. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 11/1/25)

title: Membership Committee
verbatimText: (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Composition
verbatimText: The Membership Committee shall be composed of 10 members, including one from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a); one from among the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(b); two from among the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(c); two from among the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(d); and one member of the Student-Athlete Advocacy Committee. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, Revised: 3/12/26 effective 2/25/26) 6/22/26 379

title: Duties and Responsibilities
verbatimText: The Membership Committee shall report to the Cabinet and shall oversee Division I conference and institution membership requirements. Specific duties and responsibilities are set forth below and in the Membership Committee’s policies and procedures. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, Revised: 10/22/25) (a) Oversee the reclassification policies and processes for institutions reclassifying to Division I; (b) Oversee and manage conference membership requirements; (c) Oversee and manage institutional membership requirements; and (d) Manage additional membership issues, as necessary.

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) shall have 2.75 votes each. All other representatives shall have one vote each. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Student-Athlete Advocacy Committee
verbatimText: (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, Revised: 3/12/26 effective 2/25/26)

title: Composition
verbatimText: The Student-Athlete Advocacy Committee shall consist of one student-athlete from each of the multisport conferences identified in Bylaw 21.2.1 as selected by the conference and additional ad hoc members selected by the Student-Athlete Advocacy Committee leadership and ratified by the Cabinet Subcommittee. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, Revised: 10/22/25, 3/12/26 effective 2/25/26)

title: Term of Office
verbatimText: A student-athlete member shall not serve more than two years on the committee but may request appointment for another two-year term, subject to the Cabinet Subcommittee's approval. A member may serve up to one year after completion of intercollegiate athletics participation. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, Revised: 10/22/25)

title: Duties
verbatimText: The committee shall receive information and explanations of divisional activities and legislation, review and react to topics referred to it by other governance entities and comment to the governance structure on any divisional subject of interest. Committee members shall serve on other committees as outlined in this article. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Sports Oversight Committees
verbatimText: Each oversight committee shall report to the Cabinet and shall maintain oversight of the applicable Division I championship sport. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, Revised: 10/22/25)

title: Duties and Responsibilities
verbatimText: Specific duties and responsibilities are set forth below and in the each committee’s policies and procedures. Unless otherwise noted, each sport oversight committee shall: (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, Revised: 10/22/25) (a) Maintain policies and procedures governing the administration and conduct of the championships under its jurisdiction; (b) Establish championships selection criteria, including requirements to use regular-season playing rules that conform with rules used in NCAA championships under its jurisdiction in those sports for which the Association does not maintain playing rules; (c) Select participants for and conduct the championship of the sport or sports under its jurisdiction; (d) Oversee playing rules. In sports in which the Association publishes playing rules, a rules subcommittee shall report to the corresponding oversight committee. The composition and duties of a subcommittee are set forth in the corresponding oversight committee’s policies and procedures; (e) Address future issues, challenges, opportunities and outcomes, focusing on strategic topics; (f) Adopt legislation and policies in the following areas, subject to the review of the Cabinet: (1) Sportsmanship and other ethical behaviors; (2) Conduct of athletics personnel; (3) Recruiting; (4) Playing and practice seasons; and (5) Processes and procedures related to the administration of the championship under its jurisdiction. (g) Provide feedback to relevant committees regarding the impact of policy and legislative changes in areas not listed in (f) above; (h) Perform all duties directly related to interpretations, including determining interpretations, identifying interpretations to be incorporated into the Division I Manual, modifying interpretations, reviewing interpretations issued by the NCAA staff and acting on appeals of staff interpretations of the bylaws in the areas listed in (f) above. The Cabinet shall oversee the policies and procedures for interpretations; 6/22/26 380 (i) Review and act upon appeals of NCAA staff decisions on legislative relief wavier requests related to the areas listed in (f) above and provide annual reports of legislative relief waiver decisions to the Cabinet. The Cabinet shall oversee the policies and procedures for such waiver requests; (j) Make recommendations to the Academics and Eligibility Committee on academic issues, seasons of competition and general eligibility requirements; (k) Prioritize enhancement of the student-athlete educational experience (academically and athletically) and in doing so, promote student-athletes’ personal growth and leadership development; and (l) Work in conjunction with appropriate governance entities to provide solutions to issues impacting the health and safety of student-athletes.

title: Authority
verbatimText: An interpretive or waiver decision made by a sport oversight committee shall be final, binding and conclusive and shall not be subject to further review by any other authority. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Baseball Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Baseball Oversight Committee shall include 14 voting members and four nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport; one member from each of the three highest ranked other conferences based on baseball success; three atlarge members from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport; and four student-athletes. Baseball success is defined as total conference appearances plus total conference wins in the previous five NCAA championships. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, an American Baseball Coaches Association representative, an officials coordinator and a secretary-rules editor. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) shall have 4.5 votes each. All other voting representatives shall have one vote each. Weighted voting shall only apply to regulatory and nonchampionship administrative issues. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Men's Basketball Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Men’s Basketball Oversight Committee shall include 15 voting members and five nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport and the Big East Conference (including one head coach); three members from among the six highest ranked other conferences based on basketball success (including one head coach); three at-large members from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport (including one head coach); and three student-athletes [the chair of the Men’s Basketball Student-Athlete Engagement Group, one from the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) or the Big East Conference, one at-large]. Basketball success is defined as total conference appearances plus total conference wins in the previous five NCAA championships, excluding First Four wins. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, the executive director of the National Association of Basketball Coaches, the vice chair of the Men’s Basketball Committee, the coordinator of officials and a secretary-rules editor. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) and the representative of the Big East Conference shall have three votes each. All other voting representatives shall have one vote each. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Men's Basketball Committee
verbatimText: The Men's Basketball Committee shall consist of 12 members, including one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that are eligible for automatic qualification, four members selected from the seven highest ranked other conferences based on basketball success and the remaining members from the remaining conferences. Basketball success is defined as total conference appearances plus total conference wins in the previous five NCAA championships, excluding First Four wins. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Term of Office
verbatimText: A committee member shall be appointed for a five-year term. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Women's Basketball Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Women’s Basketball Oversight Committee shall include 14 voting members and four nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport (including one head coach); three members from among the six highest ranked other conferences based on basketball success (including one head coach); four at-large members from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport (including one head coach); and three student-athletes (the chair of the Women’s Basketball Student-Athlete Engagement Group, one from the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a), one from other conferences). Basketball success is defined as total conference appearances plus total conference wins in the previous five NCAA championships, excluding First Four wins. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, the executive director of the Women’s Basketball Coaches Association, the vice chair of the Women’s Basketball Committee and a public appointment. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25) 6/22/26 381

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) shall have 4.5 votes each. All other voting representatives shall have one vote each. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Women's Basketball Committee
verbatimText: The Women's Basketball Committee shall consist of 12 members, including one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that are eligible for automatic qualification, four members selected from the seven highest ranked other conferences based on basketball success and the remaining members from the remaining conferences. Basketball success is defined as total conference appearances plus total conference wins in the previous five NCAA championships, excluding First Four wins. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Term of Office
verbatimText: A committee member shall be appointed for a five-year term. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Field Hockey Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Field Hockey Oversight Committee shall include eight voting members and four nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport; one member from each of the three highest ranked other conferences based on field hockey success; two at-large members from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport; and one student-athlete. Field hockey success is defined as total conference appearances plus total conference wins in the previous five NCAA championships. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, a coaches association representative, an officials coordinator and a secretary-rules editor. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Football Bowl Subdivision Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Football Bowl Subdivision Oversight Committee shall include 16 voting members and three nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a); one from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(b); two head coaches from any FBS conference; one commissioner from any FBS conference; one football administrator from any FBS conference; and two studentathletes (one shall be a member of the Student-Athlete Advocacy Committee). The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, a American Football Coaches Association representative and a College Football Playoff representative. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, Revised: 3/12/26 effective 2/25/26)

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) shall have 5.5 votes each. All other voting representatives shall have one vote each. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Football Championship Subdivision Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Football Championship Subdivision Oversight Committee shall include 15 voting members and three nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(c); one from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(b); and two student-athletes (one shall be a member of the Student-Athlete Advocacy Committee). The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, a American Football Coaches Association representative and the chair of the Football Championship Committee. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25, Revised: 3/12/26 effective 2/25/26)

title: Football Championship Committee
verbatimText: The Football Championship Committee shall consist of one representative from each conference that is eligible for and applies for automatic qualification for participation in the Division I Football Championship. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Men’s and Women's Golf Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Men’s and Women's Golf Oversight Committee shall include 20 voting members and three nonvoting members. The voting members shall include two members (one for men’s golf and one for women’s golf) from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport; one member from each of the three highest ranked other conferences based on men’s golf success; one member from each of the three highest ranked other conferences based on women’s golf success; one at-large member for men’s golf and one at-large member for women’s golf from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport; and four student-athletes (two men and two women). Golf success is defined as total conference appearances plus total conference wins in the previous five NCAA championships. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, a Golf Coaches Association of America representative and a Women’s Golf Coaches Association representative. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) shall have 2.75 votes each. All other voting representatives shall have one vote each. Weighted voting shall only apply to regulatory and nonchampionship administrative issues. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Men's Ice Hockey Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Men's Ice Hockey Oversight Committee shall include seven voting members and four nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport; one member from each of the three highest ranked other conferences based on ice hockey success; two at-large members from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport; and one student-athlete. Ice hockey success is defined as total conference appearances plus total conference wins in the previous five NCAA championships. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, a coaches association representative, an officials 6/22/26 382 coordinator and a secretary-rules editor. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) and those whose representation is based on success shall have 1.25 votes each. All other voting representatives shall have one vote each. Weighted voting shall only apply to regulatory and nonchampionship administrative issues. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Men's Lacrosse Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Men's Lacrosse Oversight Committee shall include eight voting members and four nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport; one member from each of the three highest ranked other conferences based on lacrosse success; two at-large members from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport; and one student-athlete. Lacrosse success is defined as total conference appearances plus total conference wins in the previous five NCAA championships. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, a Intercollegiate Men's Lacrosse Coaches Association representative, an officials coordinator and a secretary-rules editor. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Women's Lacrosse Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Women's Lacrosse Oversight Committee shall include 10 voting members and four nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport; one member from each of the three highest ranked other conferences based on lacrosse success; two at-large members from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport; and one student-athlete. Lacrosse success is defined as total conference appearances plus total conference wins in the previous five NCAA championships. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, a Intercollegiate Women's Lacrosse Coaches Association representative, an officials coordinator and a secretary-rules editor. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Women's Rowing Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Women's Rowing Oversight Committee shall include 10 voting members and two nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport; one member from each of the three highest ranked other conferences based on rowing success; two at-large members from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport; and one student-athlete. Rowing success shall be defined as the total number of points scored by a conference in the previous five NCAA championships. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative and a coaches association representative. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) shall have 2.75 votes each. All other voting representatives shall have one vote each. Weighted voting shall only apply to regulatory and nonchampionship administrative issues. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Men's Soccer Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Men's Soccer Oversight Committee shall include eight voting members and four nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport; one member from each of the three highest ranked other conferences based on soccer success; two at-large members from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport; and one student-athlete. Soccer success is defined as total conference appearances plus total conference wins in the previous five NCAA championships. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, a United Soccer Coaches Association representative, an officials coordinator and a secretary-rules editor. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) and those whose representation is based on success shall have 1.75 votes each. All other voting representatives shall have one vote each. Weighted voting shall only apply to regulatory and nonchampionship administrative issues. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Women's Soccer Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Women's Soccer Oversight Committee shall include 14 voting members and four nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport; one member from each of the three highest ranked other conferences based on soccer success; three at-large members from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport; and four student-athletes. Soccer success is defined as total conference appearances plus total conference wins in the previous five NCAA championships. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, a soccer coaches association representative, an officials coordinator and a secretary-rules editor. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) shall have 4.75 votes each. All other voting representatives shall have one vote each. Weighted voting shall only apply to regulatory and nonchampionship administrative issues. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Softball Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Softball Oversight Committee shall include 14 voting members and four nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport; one member from each of the three highest ranked other conferences based on softball success; three at- 6/22/26 383 large members from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport; and four student-athletes. Softball success is defined as total conference appearances plus total conference wins in the previous five NCAA championships. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, a National Fastpitch Coaches Association representative, an officials coordinator and a secretary-rules editor. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) shall have 4.75 votes each. All other voting representatives shall have one vote each. Weighted voting shall only apply to regulatory and nonchampionship administrative issues. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Men's and Women's Swimming and Diving Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Men's and Women's Swimming and Diving Oversight Committee shall include 15 voting members and three nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport; one member from each of the four highest ranked other conferences based on swimming and diving success; two at-large members for swimming and two at-large members for diving from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport; and three student-athletes (one men’s swimming and diving student-athlete, one women’s swimming and diving student-athlete, one diving student-athlete). Swimming and diving success is defined as the total number of points scored by a conference in the previous five NCAA championships. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, a coaches association representative and a secretary-rules editor. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) shall have five votes each. All other voting representatives shall have one vote each. Weighted voting shall only apply to regulatory and nonchampionship administrative issues. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Men's and Women's Tennis Committee
verbatimText: The Men's and Women's Tennis Committee shall include 20 voting members and three nonvoting members. The voting members shall include two members (one for men’s tennis and one for women’s tennis) from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport; one member from each of the three highest ranked other conferences based on men’s tennis success; one member from each of the three highest ranked other conferences based on women’s tennis success; one at-large member for men’s tennis and one at-large member for women’s tennis from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport; and four student-athletes (two men and two women). Tennis success is defined as total conference appearances plus total conference wins in the previous five NCAA championships. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, a coaches association representative and a head official. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) shall have 2.75 votes each. All other voting representatives shall have one vote each. Weighted voting shall only apply to regulatory and nonchampionship administrative issues. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Men's and Women's Track and Field and Cross Country Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Men's and Women's Track and Field and Cross Country Oversight Committee shall include 16 voting members and three nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport; one member from each of the four highest ranked other conferences based on track and field and cross country success; four at-large members from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport; and four student-athletes (two men and two women). Track and field and cross country success is defined as the total number of points scored by a conference in the previous five NCAA championships. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, a coaches association representative and a secretary-rules editor. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) shall have 5.5 votes each. All other voting representatives shall have one vote each. Weighted voting shall only apply to regulatory and nonchampionship administrative issues. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Women's Volleyball Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Women's Volleyball Oversight Committee shall include 14 voting members and four nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport; one member from each of the three highest ranked other conferences based on volleyball success; three at-large members from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport; and four student-athletes. Volleyball success is defined as total conference appearances plus total conference wins in the previous five NCAA championships. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, an American Volleyball Coaches Association representative, an officials coordinator and a secretary-rules editor. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Weighted Voting
verbatimText: The representatives of the conferences listed in Bylaw 21.1.1-(a) shall have 4.75 votes each. All other voting representatives shall have one vote each. Weighted voting shall only apply to regulatory and nonchampionship administrative issues. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25) 6/22/26 384

title: Men's Wrestling Oversight Committee
verbatimText: The Men's Wrestling Oversight Committee shall include eight voting members and four nonvoting members. The voting members shall include one member from each of the conferences named in Bylaw 21.2.1-(a) that sponsors the sport; one member from each of the two highest ranked other conferences based on wrestling success; two at-large members from the remaining conferences that sponsor the sport; and one student-athlete. Wrestling success is defined as total conference appearances plus total conference wins in the previous five NCAA championships. The nonvoting members shall include a faculty athletics representative, a coaches association representative, an officials coordinator and a secretary-rules editor. (Adopted: 8/5/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Selection, Term of Office and Operation
Method of Selection
verbatimText: [#] Each division's governance structure shall appoint members to serve on the general Association-wide committees of the Association, consistent with that division's committee-appointment procedures. The membership of each committee shall include representatives from each of the Association's membership divisions, including each subdivision of Division I. (Revised: 1/10/90, 1/16/93, 1/14/97 effective 8/1/97, 11/1/01, 5/13/08)

title: Term of Office
verbatimText: [#] Unless otherwise specified, a member of the committees listed in this bylaw shall be appointed for one four-year term. A former member may be appointed to an additional term after three years have elapsed. An individual who has served two terms on a committee may not serve further on that committee. A member's term of service shall commence on the first day of September following the member's election or appointment. (Revised: 5/13/08)

title: Operation
verbatimText: [#] The Board of Governors also shall ratify policies regarding length, location and expenses of Association-wide committee meetings. (Revised: 1/10/90, 1/16/93, 1/14/97 effective 8/1/97, 11/1/01, 5/13/08, 10/30/14)

title: Committee on Competitive Safeguards and Medical Aspects of Sports
Composition
verbatimText: [#] The Committee on Competitive Safeguards and Medical Aspects of Sports shall consist of 25 members, including seven positions allocated for men, seven allocated for women and 11 unallocated. The membership of the committee shall be constituted as follows: (Revised: 1/10/90, 1/10/91, 1/10/95, 10/28/99, 4/26/01, 11/1/01, 10/30/03, 7/22/14, 4/30/15, 3/27/17, 4/14/17, 10/4/17, 5/18/22, 2/17/23, 1/15/25, 1/28/26) (a) Two athletics directors or senior woman athletics administrators, one man and one woman; (b) One member who is an active coach; (c) One strength and conditioning specialist certified by an accredited strength and conditioning certification body; (d) Five members from the field of medicine, of whom at least two shall be women. One shall be a primary-care physician who is board certified in family practice, internal medicine or emergency medicine, and shall have a current Certificate of Added Qualifications in Sports Medicine. One shall be a board certified orthopedic surgeon. One shall be a physician with expertise in endocrinology (who may be a member of the general public). One shall be a physician with expertise in drug-testing. One shall be a physician who is a member of the general public; (e) Two athletic trainers, one man and one woman. One must be responsible for the total athletics training program at a member institution; (f) One member representing the field of law who is a lawyer practicing in sports law or a related field, or a faculty member in sports law or a related field; (g) One member of the NCAA Football Rules Committee; (h) One member representing secondary school interests. This individual shall be the National Federation of State High School Associations’ staff liaison to the Sports Medicine Advisory Board and shall be eligible for reappointment without restriction; (i) One member active in sport-science research; (j) One member with expertise in the area of drug education; (k) One student-athlete from each division (who shall have one vote each) shall serve as a member of the committee. Each student-athlete may serve on the committee up to one year after completion of intercollegiate athletics eligibility; (l) Two licensed mental health practitioners, one man and one woman; (m) One registered dietitian who specializes in sports nutrition; (n) One representative from the Division I governance structure; (o) One representative from the Division II Management Council; and (p) One representative from the Division III Management Council. 6/22/26 385

title: Duties
verbatimText: [#] The committee, subject to the direction of the Board of Governors, and in conjunction with the NCAA Sport Science Institute, shall: (Revised: 10/31/02, 10/30/14) (a) Promote and sponsor research to address relevant health and safety issues; (b) Promote education to enhance the health and safety of student-athletes; (c) Operate a national injury surveillance program to monitor injury trends and enhance safety in intercollegiate athletics; (d) Deter the use of NCAA banned substances in order to promote fair competition and safety; (e) Facilitate outreach activities to enhance student-athlete health and safety; and (f) Provide a health and safety perspective on relevant legislation and policy.

title: Term of Office, Chair
verbatimText: [#] The chair may remain on the committee for up to one additional year if the individual becomes the committee chair with only one year or less remaining on the individual's four-year term. (Adopted: 1/9/06 effective 10/22/07)

title: Committee on Access, Opportunity and Impact
verbatimText: [#] (Adopted: 1/15/25 effective 9/1/25)

title: Composition
